volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 221a - Section 333)

sections.label: Section 221a
sections.translation: O Zeus !

sections.label: Section 222
sections.translation: — and the pitiful Oedipus ?

sections.label: Section 222a
sections.translation: You are he ?

sections.label: Section 223
sections.translation: Have no fear of any words that I speak —

sections.label: Section 224
sections.translation: Ah , no , no !

sections.label: Section 224a
sections.translation: Unhappy that I am !

sections.label: Section 224b
sections.translation: Oh , oh !

sections.label: Section 225
sections.translation: Daughter , what is about to happen ?

sections.label: Section 226
sections.translation: Out with you ! Go forth from the land !

sections.label: Section 227
sections.translation: And your promise — to what fulfillment will you bring it ?

sections.label: Section 228
sections.translation: No man is visited by the punishment of fate if he requites deeds which were first done to himself .

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: ἃν προπάθῃ τὸ τίνειν · ἀπάτα δʼ ἀπά-
sections.translation: Deceit on the one part matches deceits on the other , and gives pain instead of pleasure for reward . And you — back with you ! Out from your seat !

sections.label: Section 235
sections.translation: Away from my land with all speed , that you may not fasten some heavier burden on my city !

sections.label: Section 237
sections.translation: Reverent strangers , since you have not endured my aged father — knowing , as you do ,

sections.label: Section 240
sections.translation: the rumor of his unintended deeds — pity at least my poor self , I implore you , who supplicate you for my father alone . I beg you with eyes that can still look

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: ὄμμα σὸν ὄμμασιν , ὥς τις ἀφʼ αἵματος
sections.translation:
on your own , like one sprung from your own blood , that this sufferer may meet with reverent treatment .
On you , as on a god , we depend in our misery . But come , grant the favor for which we hardly dare hope !

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: πρός σʼ ὅ τι σοι φίλον ἐκ σέθεν ἄντομαι ,
sections.translation: I implore you by everything that you hold dear at home : by child , by wife , or treasure , or god ! Look well and you will not find the mortal who , if a god should lead him on , could escape .

sections.label: Section 254
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἴσθι , τέκνον Οἰδίπου , σέ τʼ ἐξ ἴσου
sections.translation: Feel sure , daughter of Oedipus , that we pity you and him alike

sections.label: Section 255
sections.translation: for your misfortune ; but dreading the punishment of the gods , we could not say anything beyond what we have now said to you .

sections.label: Section 259
sections.translation: What help comes , then , of repute or fair fame , if it ends in idle breath ;

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: εἰ τάς γʼ Ἀθήνας φασὶ θεοσεβεστάτας
sections.translation:
seeing that Athens , as men say , is god-fearing beyond all , and alone has the power to shelter the outraged stranger , and alone the power to help him ?
And where are these things for me , when , after making me rise up from this rocky seat , you then drive me from the land , afraid of my name alone ?

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: ὄνομα μόνον δείσαντες ; οὐ γὰρ δὴ τό γε
sections.translation: Not , surely , afraid of my person or of my acts ; since my acts , at least , have been in suffering rather than doing — if I must mention the tale of my mother and my father , because of which you fear me . That know I full well .

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: ἔξοιδα . καίτοι πῶς ἐγὼ κακὸς φύσιν ,
sections.translation: And yet how was I innately evil ? I , who was merely requiting a wrong , so that , had I been acting with knowledge , even then I could not be accounted evil . But , as it was , all unknowing I went where I went — while they who wronged me knowingly sought my ruin .

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: ἀνθʼ ὧν ἱκνοῦμαι πρὸς θεῶν ὑμᾶς , ξένοι ,
sections.translation: Therefore , strangers , I beseech you by the gods : just as you made me leave my seat , so protect me , and do not , while you render honor to the gods , consider those gods to be fools . But rather consider that they look on the god-fearing man

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: βλέπειν δὲ πρὸς τοὺς δυσσεβεῖς , φυγὴν δέ του
sections.translation:
and on the godless , and that never yet has an impious man found escape .
With the help of those gods , do not becloud the prosperity of Athens by paying service to unholy deeds . As you have received the suppliant under your pledge ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: ῥύου με κἀκφύλασσε · μηδέ μου κάρα
sections.translation: rescue me and guard me to the end ; nor dishonor me when you look on this face unlovely to behold , for I have come to you as one sacred and pious , bearing comfort for this people . But when the master has come ,

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: τότʼ εἰσακούων πάντʼ ἐπιστήσει · τὰ δὲ
sections.translation: whoever is your leader , then you will hear and know all ; meanwhile show yourselves in no way evil .

sections.label: Section 292
sections.translation: The thoughts you urge , old man , must move awe ; they have been set forth in grave words .

sections.label: Section 295
sections.translation: But I am content that the rulers of our country should judge in this case .

sections.label: Section 296
sections.original: καὶ ποῦ ʼσθʼ ὁ κραίνων τῆσδε τῆς χώρας , ξένοι ;
sections.translation: And where , strangers , is the lord of this realm ?

sections.label: Section 297
sections.original: πατρῷον ἄστυ γῆς ἔχει · σκοπὸς δέ νιν ,
sections.translation: He is at the city of his fathers in our land . The messenger who sent us here has gone to fetch him .

sections.label: Section 299
sections.original: ἦ καὶ δοκεῖτε τοῦ τυφλοῦ τινʼ ἐντροπὴν
sections.translation: Do you think that he will have any regard or care for the blind man ,

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: ἢ φροντίδʼ , ἕξειν , αὐτὸν ὥστʼ ἐλθεῖν πέλας ;
sections.translation: so as to come here himself ?

sections.label: Section 301
sections.original: καὶ κάρθʼ , ὅταν περ τοὔνομʼ αἴσθηται τὸ σόν .
sections.translation: Yes , surely , as soon as he learns of your name .

sections.label: Section 302
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἔσθʼ ὁ κείνῳ τοῦτο τοὔπος ἀγγελῶν ;
sections.translation: Who is there to bring him that word ?

sections.label: Section 303
sections.original: μακρὰ κέλευθος · πολλὰ δʼ ἐμπόρων ἔπη
sections.translation: The way is long , and many words from travellers often wander about . When he hears them , he will soon be with us , never fear .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: θάρσει , παρέσται . πολὺ γάρ , ὦ γέρον , τὸ σὸν
sections.translation: For your name , old man , has been loudly trumpeted through all lands , so that even if he is taking his ease , and slow to move , when he hears of you he will swiftly arrive .

sections.label: Section 308
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὐτυχὴς ἵκοιτο τῇ θʼ αὑτοῦ πόλει
sections.translation: Well , may he come with good fortune both for his own city and for me ! What noble man is not his own friend ?

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , τί λέξω ; ποῖ φρενῶν ἔλθω , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: O Zeus ! What shall I say ? What shall I think , my father ?

sections.label: Section 311
sections.translation: What is it , Antigone , my child ?

sections.label: Section 311a
sections.translation: I see a woman coming towards us , mounted on a colt of Etna ; she wears a Thessalian bonnet to screen her face from the sun .

sections.label: Section 315
sections.translation: What shall I say ? Is it she , or is it not ? Does my judgment err ? Yes — no — I cannot tell — ah , me ! It is no other , yes ! She greets me with bright glances

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: σαίνει με προσστείχουσα · σημαίνει δʼ ὅτι
sections.translation: as she draws near , and makes a signal . Here is Ismene , clearly , and no other before me .

sections.label: Section 322
sections.translation: What is that you say , my child ?

sections.label: Section 322a
sections.translation: That I see your daughter , my sister . By her voice right away you can know her .

sections.label: Section 324
sections.original: ὦ δισσὰ πατρὸς καὶ κασιγνήτης ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: Father and sister , names most sweet to me ! How hard it was to find you !

sections.label: Section 325
sections.translation: And how hard now to look upon you for my tears !

sections.label: Section 327
sections.translation: My child , have you come ?

sections.label: Section 327a
sections.translation: Father , your fate is sad to see !

sections.label: Section 328
sections.translation: Are you with us , my child ?

sections.label: Section 328a
sections.translation: Not without toil , indeed , for myself .

sections.label: Section 329
sections.translation: Touch me , my daughter !

sections.label: Section 329a
sections.translation: I give a hand to each at once .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.translation: Ah my children , my sisters !

sections.label: Section 330a
sections.translation: Alas , twice-wretched life !

sections.label: Section 331
sections.translation: Her life and mine ?

sections.label: Section 331a
sections.translation: And mine , wretched me , makes a third .

sections.label: Section 332
sections.translation: Child , why have you come ?

sections.label: Section 332a
sections.translation: Through concern for you , father .

sections.label: Section 333
sections.translation: Through longing to see me ?
